items.title: Somalia: Somaliland Leader Meets Israeli Defence Minister As Both Sides Signal Deeper Security Ties
items.description: [Shabelle] Jerusalem -- Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Tuesday that Israel and Somaliland had cooperated for years on a series of undisclosed operations, as he hosted Somaliland President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi Irro for talks focused on expanding security cooperation.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somaliland Leader Meets Israeli Defence Minister As Both Sides Signal Deeper Security Ties

items.title: Somalia: Somalia, Egypt Hold Aid Talks in Mogadishu
items.description: [Shabelle] Mogadishu -- Somalia's disaster management chief met Wednesday with a senior Egyptian official in Mogadishu to strengthen humanitarian cooperation.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somalia, Egypt Hold Aid Talks in Mogadishu

items.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
items.description: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.
items.titleIt: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky

items.title: Somaliland opens embassy in Jerusalem after historic Israeli recognition
items.description: For Somaliland, the embassy opening represents a diplomatic victory and could strengthen its campaign for broader international recognition. For Israel, it marks the deepening of ties with a strategic partner in the Horn of Africa.
items.titleIt: Somaliland opens embassy in Jerusalem after historic Israeli recognition

items.title: Sisi hosts Sheikh Mohamed, welcomes deal to end Iran war
items.description: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi and UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed met in Cairo to strengthen strategic ties and discuss regional security. The leaders welcomed the end of the Iran war and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz while calling for greater Arab coordination.
items.titleIt: Sisi hosts Sheikh Mohamed, welcomes deal to end Iran war

items.title: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said Wednesday his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi had assured him of the safe departure of an Italian ship, the Grimaldi Group's car carrier Grande Torino, that has been blocked in the Persian Gulf for months. Araghchi, said Tajani on X, reporting on a phone call with his counterpart, "assured me that in the next few hours the Iranian authorities will ensure the conditions for the safe departure of the Italian Grimaldi ship 'Grande Torino,'" "blocked for months in the Gulf." (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Tajani says Araghchi assured him of safe departure of Grimaldi ship

items.title: Africa: 'The Challenge Is No Longer Identifying Solutions. It Is Scaling Them' #OOC11
items.description: [allAfrica] Mombasa, Kenya -- As Africa hosts the Our Ocean Conference (OOC11) for the first time, The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is spotlighting its deep partnership with African governments and coastal communities to translate ocean commitments into tangible, on-the-ground impact. The world has committed to protecting 30% of the ocean by 2030, but the real test is whether those promises are being turned into action. Attention is turning to what meaningful progress looks like on paper and in practice.
items.titleIt: Africa: 'The Challenge Is No Longer Identifying Solutions. It Is Scaling Them' #OOC11

items.title: G7 Turns spotlight on Middle East as Trump unveils Iran deal
items.description: G7 leaders and invited partner nations gathered in Evian-les-Bains as attention shifted from Ukraine to the Middle East following Donald Trump’s announcement of a tentative deal with Iran, with Gulf leaders joining talks on regional security and global economic risks.

items.title: Italy to reopen Tehran embassy on Friday says Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani told a question time session in parliament on Wednesday that Italy will reopen its embassy in Tehran on Friday after Iran and the United States reached an agreement to end the war in the Middle East. Premier Giorgia Meloni's government has already said Italy is willing to take part in an international naval mission for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. (ANSA). Read article...
items.titleIt: Italy to reopen Tehran embassy on Friday says Tajani

items.title: Remigration is not a 'Christian response' says Pope Leo XIV
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 17 - Pope Leo XIV has said that remigration, a policy being proposed by several far-right parties, including former General Roberto Vannacci's new National Future group in Italy, is not a concept that complies with Christian principles. "Many times we do not recognize the reasons why these people had to leave their countries," the American pontiff told reporters outside the papal residence in Castel Gandolf on Tuesday in relation to remigration. "So many reasons: violence, war, conflicts. And so simply saying, 'Let's send them away, so we can wash our hands of the problem' d
items.titleIt: Remigration is not a 'Christian response' says Pope Leo XIV
